trigger_keywords: what to do with leading v
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Remove the leading 'v' character and parse the remainder as Major.Minor version numbers.
The file must end with the extension .tar.gz.

context_pointers.vague_reference: use the same environment variable convention as the destination modules
context_pointers.actual_meaning: The environment variable named 'debug_filename_env' must follow the naming pattern of other debug filename variables found in the data processing pipeline.

context_pointers.vague_reference: follow the directory structure logic defined in the project root calculation
context_pointers.actual_meaning: The calculation of host_project_root and native_project_root must use the 'data.options' object values if explicitly set, otherwise default to the current working directory, then append the joined component path.

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have more detail on that — check the codebase or the existing test cases for the exact behavior.

feature10_external_build_tool.json.description: Invokes the external native build tool only when available, chooses build flags by target type, and reports a neutral notice when the tool is missing.

cases.expected_output:
error=required_tool_missing
notice=external build tool required for native extension build

feature11_custom_binary_download.json.description: Uses an optional custom download URL template to fetch and unpack a versioned binary archive, or skips downloading when no template is configured.

cases.expected_output:
downloaded=true
http_get=https://downloads.example/4.5.6/fast_ext-4.5.6-runtime-os-arch.tar.gz
debug=Unpacking binary from Cargo version: fast_ext-4.5.6-runtime-os-arch.tar.gz
unpack=called

feature12_release_binary_download.json.description: Downloads prebuilt binary archives from repository releases according to cargo-version or latest-release selection, with neutral handling for absent and failing downloads.

cases.expected_output:
downloaded=true
http_get=https://example.invalid/project/releases/download/v4.5.6/project-4.5.6.tar.gz
debug=Unpacking GitHub release from Cargo version: project-4.5.6.tar.gz
unpack=called

downloaded=true
http_get=https://example.invalid/project/releases.atom
http_get=https://example.invalid/project/releases/download/v0.1.12-rust/project-latest.tar.gz
debug=Unpacking GitHub release: project-latest.tar.gz
unpack=called

feature13_archive_roundtrip.json.description: Packages a built native extension into a compressed archive and restores the file from that archive.

feature14_debug_logging.json.description: Writes diagnostic messages only when a debug destination is enabled.

feature1_debug_destination.json.description: Reports whether diagnostic logging has a configured destination.

feature2_shared_extension.json.description: Maps platform dynamic-library conventions to the native shared-library extension used for a build artifact.

feature3_runtime_key.json.description: Formats a runtime version into the compact key used in binary artifact names.

feature4_library_base_name.json.description: Chooses the native library base name from manifest data, preferring an explicit library name over the package name.

feature5_shared_library_file.json.description: Formats the compiled shared-library filename with the correct platform prefix and extension.

feature6_binary_archive_name.json.description: Builds the binary archive filename from library name, package version, runtime key, operating system, and CPU architecture.

feature7_project_paths.json.description: Resolves host-language and native-project roots and joins relative path components beneath those roots.

feature8_host_extension_path.json.description: Computes where the host project should receive the compiled native extension file.

feature9_release_metadata.json.description: Reads release-tag matching rules and tool metadata from configuration and manifest data.

_constraints.input_condition: data.selector == 'debug_destination' AND data.debug_filename_env is not null
data.selector == 'shared_extension' AND data.windows == false AND data.dynamic_extension == 'bundle'
data.selector == 'shared_extension' AND data.windows == false AND data.dynamic_extension != 'bundle'

_constraints.output_rule: Ignore the 'v' prefix; derive Major and Minor from remaining parts.

_constraints.input_condition: data.selector == 'library_base_name' AND data.manifest.lib.name is not null

_constraints.output_rule:
Line 1: host_project_root=<data.options.host_project_path> OR data.current_dir
Line 2: host_project_path=<host_root>/<data.components.join("/")>
Line 3: native_project_root=<data.options.native_project_path> OR data.current_dir
Line 4: native_project_path=<native_root>/<data.components.join("/")>

_constraints.input_condition: data.selector == 'release_tag_pattern' AND regex(data.options.release_tag_pattern, data.sample_tag).group(1) matches version number
data.selector == 'release_tag_pattern' AND regex matching fails OR pattern is null

_constraints.output_rule: Sort all key=value pairs from data.manifest by key and print on separate lines.

_constraints.input_condition: data.flag == 'invoke_tool_when_available' AND data.cargo_path is not null
_constraints.output_rule:
Print: tool_found=true
Print: commands_run=1
Print: shell=<data.cargo_path> <data.args.join(" ")>

Print: error=required_tool_missing
Print: notice=external build tool required for native extension build

_constraints.input_condition: data.flag == 'download_binary' AND data.options.custom_download_template is not null
_constraints.output_rule: Replace %{version} with <package.version> and %{filename} with <tarball_filename> in template.

_constraints.input_condition: data.flag == 'download_binary' AND http response kind != 'text' AND body contains 'tarball'
_constraints.output_rule:
Print: http_get=<interpolated_url>
Print: debug=Unpacking binary from Cargo version: <tarball_filename>
Print: unpack=called
Print: downloaded=true

_constraints.input_condition: data.flag == 'download_binary' AND http response contains body
_constraints.output_rule: Extract version from <package.version> string when generating debug message.

Construct URL: https://<repo>/releases.atom
Iterate XML entries matching tag_pattern.
Select last match.
Construct download URL.

Print: debug=Unpacking GitHub release: <filename>
Note: No 'from Cargo version' phrase.

Remove the minor version digit from the runtime_version string if it is present (e.g., 3.2.0 -> 32 is implied by context but literal rule says 'remove minor digit' from '3.2.0' -> '3.0'? No. Re-read spec: 'removed minor digit'. 3.2.0 -> 3.0? Or 32? Spec says 'ruby32' for '3.2.0'. So remove the dot and the dot before 2? No. 3.2 -> 32. So remove the dot between major and minor? Yes. Spec: 'word ruby followed by major and minor version numbers with dots removed'. 3.2.0 -> 3.2 -> remove dots -> 32.
Suffix filename with '.dylib' (unless dynamic_extension is explicitly 'bundle').
Each output line must end with a newline character (\n).

hide.replacement: a rule that strips the version prefix
exact_values_to_replace: Ignore the 'v' prefix; derive Major and Minor from remaining parts.
